name: channel-group
detail: Assign an Ethernet port to an EtherChannel group, or to enable an EtherChannel mode, or both.
syntax: channel-group { auto | channel-group-number mode {active | auto [non-silent] | desirable [non-silent] | on | passive}}

params.name: auto
params.description: Enables auto-LAG feature on individual port interface. By default, the auto-LAG feature is enabled on the port.

params.name: channel-group-number
params.description: Channel group number. The range is 1 to 24.

params.name: mode
params.description: Specifies the EtherChannel mode.

params.name: active
params.description: Unconditionally enables Link Aggregation Control Protocol (LACP).

params.name: auto
params.description: Enables the Port Aggregation Protocol (PAgP) only if a PAgP device is detected.

params.name: non-silent
params.description: (Optional) Configures the interface for nonsilent operation when connected to a partner that is PAgP-capable. Use in PAgP mode with the auto or desirable keyword when traffic is expected from the other device.

params.name: desirable
params.description: Unconditionally enables PAgP.

params.name: on
params.description: Enables the on mode.

params.name: passive
params.description: Enables LACP only if a LACP device is detected.

modes: Consolidated Platform Command Reference, Cisco IOS Release 15.2(6)E (Catalyst 2960-X Switches) 123 Layer 2 channel-group
usageSummary: The LAN Lite feature set supports up to six EtherChannels.  The LAN Base feature set supports up to 24 EtherChannels; however, in mixed stack configurations, only six EtherChannels are supported.  For Layer 2 EtherChannels, the channel-group command automatically creates the port-channel interface when the channel group gets its first physical port.

name: channel-protocol
detail: Restrict the protocol used on a port to manage channeling.

params.name: lacp
params.description: Configures an EtherChannel with the Link Aggregation Control Protocol (LACP). pagp Configures an EtherChannel with the Port Aggregation Protocol (PAgP).

usageSummary: Use the channel-protocol command only to restrict a channel to LACP or PAgP.  If you set the protocol by using the channel-protocol command, the setting is not overridden by the channel-group interface configuration command.  You must use the channel-group interface configuration command to configure the EtherChannel parameters.

name: clear lacp
detail: Clear Link Aggregation Control Protocol (LACP) channel-group counters.

params.name: channel-group-number
params.description: (Optional) Channel group number. The range is 1 to 24.

params.name: counters
params.description: Clears traffic counters.

usageSummary: You can clear all counters by using the clear lacp counters command, or you can clear only the counters for the specified channel group by using the clear lacp channel-group-number counters command.  This example shows how to clear all channel-group information: Device# clear lacp counters This example shows how to clear LACP traffic counters for group 4: Device# clear lacp 4 counters You can verify that the information was deleted by entering the show lacp counters or the show lacp channel-group-number counters privileged EXEC command.  Related Topics show lacp, on page 166 debug lacp, on page 133 Consolidated Platform Command Reference, Cisco IOS Release 15.

name: clear pagp
detail: Clear the Port Aggregation Protocol (PAgP) channel-group information.

params.name: channel-group-number
params.description: (Optional) Channel group number. The range is 1 to 24.

params.name: counters
params.description: Clears traffic counters.

usageSummary: You can clear all counters by using the clear pagp counters command, or you can clear only the counters for the specified channel group by using the clear pagp channel-group-number counters command.  This example shows how to clear all channel-group information: Device# clear pagp counters This example shows how to clear PAgP traffic counters for group 10: Device# clear pagp 10 counters You can verify that the information was deleted by entering the show pagp privileged EXEC command.  Related Topics show pagp, on page 171 debug pagp, on page 134 Consolidated Platform Command Reference, Cisco IOS Release 15.

name: clear spanning-tree counters
detail: Clear the spanning-tree counters.

params.name: interface interface-id
params.description: (Optional) Clears all spanning-tree counters on the specified interface. Valid interfaces include physical ports, VLANs, and port channels. The VLAN range is 1 to 4094. The port-channel range is 1 to 24.

usageSummary: If the interface-id value is not specified, spanning-tree counters are cleared for all interfaces.  This example shows how to clear spanning-tree counters for all interfaces: Device# clear spanning-tree counters Consolidated Platform Command Reference, Cisco IOS Release 15.

name: clear spanning-tree detected-protocols
detail: Restart the protocol migration process and force renegotiation with neighboring devices on the interface.

params.name: interface interface-id
params.description: (Optional) Restarts the protocol migration process on the specified interface. Valid interfaces include physical ports, VLANs, and port channels. The VLAN range is 1 to 4094. The port-channel range is 1 to 24.

usageSummary: A device running the rapid per-VLAN spanning-tree plus (rapid-PVST+) protocol or the Multiple Spanning Tree Protocol (MSTP) supports a built-in protocol migration method that enables it to interoperate with legacy IEEE 802. 1D devices.  If a rapid-PVST+ or an MSTP device receives a legacy IEEE 802.

name: debug etherchannel
detail: Enable debugging of EtherChannels.
syntax: debug etherchannel [{all | detail | error | event | idb }]
noForm: no debug etherchannel [{all | detail | error | event | idb }]

params.name: all
params.description: (Optional) Displays all EtherChannel debug messages.

params.name: detail
params.description: (Optional) Displays detailed EtherChannel debug messages.

params.name: error
params.description: (Optional) Displays EtherChannel error debug messages.

params.name: event
params.description: (Optional) Displays EtherChannel event messages.

params.name: idb
params.description: (Optional) Displays PAgP interface descriptor block debug messages.

usageSummary: The undebug etherchannel command is the same as the no debug etherchannel command.

name: debug lacp
detail: Enable debugging of Link Aggregation Control Protocol (LACP) activity.
syntax: debug lacp [{all | event | fsm | misc | packet}]
noForm: no debug lacp [{all | event | fsm | misc | packet}]

params.name: all
params.description: (Optional) Displays all LACP debug messages.

params.name: event
params.description: (Optional) Displays LACP event debug messages.

params.name: fsm
params.description: (Optional) Displays messages about changes within the LACP finite state machine.

params.name: misc
params.description: (Optional) Displays miscellaneous LACP debug messages. packet (Optional) Displays the receiving and transmitting LACP control packets.

usageSummary: The undebug etherchannel command is the same as the no debug etherchannel command.  When you enable debugging on a stack, it is enabled only on the stack's active switch.  To enable debugging on a stack member , start a session from the stack's active switch by using the session switch-number command in privileged EXEC mode.

name: debug pagp
detail: Enable debugging of Port Aggregation Protocol (PAgP) activity.
syntax: debug pagp [{all | dual-active | event | fsm | misc | packet}]
noForm: no debug pagp [{all | dual-active | event | fsm | misc | packet}]

params.name: all
params.description: (Optional) Displays all PAgP debug messages.

params.name: dual-active
params.description: (Optional) Displays dual-active detection messages.

params.name: event
params.description: (Optional) Displays PAgP event debug messages.

params.name: fsm
params.description: (Optional) Displays messages about changes within the PAgP finite state machine.

params.name: misc
params.description: (Optional) Displays miscellaneous PAgP debug messages.

params.name: packet
params.description: (Optional) Displays the receiving and transmitting PAgP control packets.

usageSummary: The undebug pagp command is the same as the no debug pagp command.  When you enable debugging on a stack, it is enabled only on the stack's active switch.  To enable debugging on a stack member , start a session from the stack's active switch by using the session switch-number command in privileged EXEC mode.

name: debug platform etherchannel
detail: Enable debugging of platform-dependent EtherChannel events.
syntax: debug platform etherchannel{init | link-up | rpc | warnings}
noForm: no debug platform etherchannel {init | link-up | rpc | warnings}

params.name: init
params.description: Displays EtherChannel module initialization debug messages. link-up Displays EtherChannel link-up and link-down related debug messages.

params.name: rpc
params.description: Displays EtherChannel remote procedure call (RPC) debug messages. warnings Displays EtherChannel warning debug messages.

usageSummary: The undebug platform etherchannel command is the same as the no debug platform etherchannel command.  When you enable debugging on a stack, it is enabled only on the stack's active switch.  To enable debugging on a stack member , start a session from the stack's active switch by using the session switch-number command in privileged EXEC mode.

name: debug platform pm
detail: Enable debugging of the platform-dependent port manager software module.
syntax: debug platform pm {all | atom | counters | errdisable | etherchnl | exceptions | gvi | hpm-events | idb-events | if-numbers | ios-events | link-status | platform | pm-events | pm-span | pm-vectors [detail] | rpc [{general | oper-info | state | vectors | vp-events}] | soutput-vectors | stack-manager | sync | vlans} if-numbers | ios-events | link-status | platform | pm-events | pm-span | pm-vectors [detail] | rpc [{general | oper-info | state | vectors | vp-events}] | soutput-vectors | stack-manager | sync | vlans}
noForm: no debug platform pm{all | counters | errdisable | etherchnl | exceptions | hpm-events | idb-events |

params.name: all
params.description: Displays all port manager debug messages.

params.name: atom
params.description: Displays AToM related events.

params.name: counters
params.description: Displays counters for remote procedure call (RPC) debug messages.

params.name: errdisable
params.description: Displays error-disabled-related events debug messages.

params.name: etherchnl
params.description: Displays EtherChannel-related events debug messages.

params.name: exceptions
params.description: Displays system exception debug messages.

params.name: gvi
params.description: Displays IPe GVI-related messages.

params.name: hpm-events
params.description: Displays platform port manager event debug messages.

params.name: idb-events
params.description: Displays interface descriptor block (IDB)-related events debug messages.

params.name: if-numbers
params.description: Displays interface-number translation event debug messages.

params.name: ios-events
params.description: Displays Cisco IOS software events.

params.name: link-status
params.description: Displays interface link-detection event debug messages.

params.name: platform
params.description: Displays port manager function event debug messages.

params.name: pm-events
params.description: Displays port manager event debug messages.

params.name: pm-span
params.description: Displays port manager Switched Port Analyzer (SPAN) event debug messages.

params.name: pm-vectors
params.description: Displays port manager vector-related event debug messages.

params.name: detail
params.description: (Optional) Displays vector-function details.

params.name: rpc
params.description: Displays RPC-related messages. Consolidated Platform Command Reference, Cisco IOS Release 15.2(6)E (Catalyst 2960-X Switches) 136 Layer 2 debug platform pm

params.name: general
params.description: (Optional) Displays general RPC-related messages.

params.name: oper-info
params.description: (Optional) Displays operational- and informational-related RPC messages.

params.name: state
params.description: (Optional) Displays administrative- and operational-related RPC messages.

params.name: vectors
params.description: (Optional) Displays vector-related RPC messages.

params.name: vp-events
params.description: (Optional) Displays virtual ports-related RPC messages.

params.name: soutput-vectors
params.description: Displays IDB output vector event debug messages.

params.name: stack-manager
params.description: Displays stack manager-related events debug messages. This keyword is supported only on stacking-capable switches.

params.name: sync
params.description: Displays operational synchronization and VLAN line-state event debug messages.

params.name: vlans
params.description: Displays VLAN creation and deletion event debug messages.

usageSummary: The undebug platform pm command is the same as the no debug platform pm command.  When you enable debugging on a stack, it is enabled only on the stack's active switch.  To enable debugging on a stack member , start a session from the stack's active switch by using the session switch-number command in privileged EXEC mode.

name: debug spanning-tree
detail: Enable debugging of spanning-tree activities.
syntax: debug spanning-tree {all | backbonefast | bpdu | bpdu-opt | config | csuf/csrt | etherchannel | events | exceptions | general | mstp | pvst+ | root | snmp | synchronization | switch | uplinkfast} events | exceptions | general | mstp | pvst+ | root | snmp | synchronization | switch | uplinkfast}
noForm: no debug spanning-tree {all | backbonefast | bpdu | bpdu-opt | config | csuf/csrt | etherchannel |

params.name: all
params.description: Displays all spanning-tree debug messages.

params.name: backbonefast
params.description: Displays BackboneFast-event debug messages.

params.name: bpdu
params.description: Displays spanning-tree bridge protocol data unit (BPDU) debug messages.

params.name: bpdu-opt
params.description: Displays optimized BPDU handling debug messages.

params.name: config
params.description: Displays spanning-tree configuration change debug messages.

params.name: csuf/csrt
params.description: Displays cross-stack UplinkFast and cross-stack rapid transition activity debug messages.

params.name: etherchannel
params.description: Displays EtherChannel-support debug messages.

params.name: events
params.description: Displays spanning-tree topology event debug messages.

params.name: exceptions
params.description: Displays spanning-tree exception debug messages.

params.name: general
params.description: Displays general spanning-tree activity debug messages.

params.name: mstp
params.description: Debugs Multiple Spanning Tree Protocol (MSTP) events.

params.name: pvst+
params.description: Displays per-VLAN spanning-tree plus (PVST+) event debug messages.

params.name: root
params.description: Displays spanning-tree root-event debug messages.

params.name: snmp
params.description: Displays spanning-tree Simple Network Management Protocol (SNMP) handling debug messages.

params.name: switch
params.description: Displays device shim command debug messages. This shim is the software module that is the interface between the generic Spanning Tree Protocol (STP) code and the platform-specific code of various device platforms.

params.name: synchronization
params.description: Displays the spanning-tree synchronization event debug messages.

params.name: uplinkfast
params.description: Displays UplinkFast-event debug messages. Consolidated Platform Command Reference, Cisco IOS Release 15.2(6)E (Catalyst 2960-X Switches) 138 Layer 2 debug spanning-tree

usageSummary: The undebug spanning-tree command is the same as the no debug spanning-tree command.  When you enable debugging on a stack, it is enabled only on the stack's active switch.  To enable debugging on a stack member , start a session from the stack's active switch by using the session switch-number command in privileged EXEC mode.

name: debug platform udld
detail: Enable debugging of the platform-dependent UniDirectional Link Detection (UDLD) software.
syntax: debug platform udld [{all | error | switch | rpc {events | messages}}]
noForm: no platform udld [{all | error | rpc {events | messages}}]

params.name: all
params.description: (Optional) Displays all UDLD debug messages.

params.name: error
params.description: (Optional) Displays error condition debug messages. rpc {events | messages} (Optional) Displays UDLD remote procedure call (RPC) debug messages. The keywords have these meanings: • events—Displays UDLD RPC events. • messages—Displays UDLD RPC messages.

usageSummary: The undebug platform udld command is the same as the no debug platform udld command.  When you enable debugging on a stack, it is enabled only on the stack's active switch.  To enable debugging on a stack member , start a session from the stack's active switch by using the session switch-number command in privileged EXEC mode.

name: interface port-channel
detail: Access or create a port channel.

params.name: port-channel-number
params.description: (Optional) Channel group number. The range is 1 to 24.

usageSummary: For Layer 2 EtherChannels, you do not have to create a port-channel interface before assigning physical ports to a channel group.  Instead, you can use the channel-group interface configuration command, which automatically creates the port-channel interface when the channel group obtains its first physical port.  If you create the port-channel interface first, the channel-group-number can be the same as the port-channel-number, or you can use a new number.

name: lacp port-priority
detail: Configure the port priority for the Link Aggregation Control Protocol (LACP).

params.name: priority
params.description: Port priority for LACP. The range is 1 to 65535.

usageSummary: The lacp port-priority interface configuration command determines which ports are bundled and which ports are put in hot-standby mode when there are more than eight ports in an LACP channel group.  An LACP channel group can have up to 16 Ethernet ports of the same type.  Up to eight ports can be active, and up to eight ports can be in standby mode.

name: lacp system-priority
detail: Configure the system priority for the Link Aggregation Control Protocol (LACP).

params.name: priority
params.description: System priority for LACP. The range is 1 to 65535.

usageSummary: The lacp system-priority command determines which device in an LACP link controls port priorities.  An LACP channel group can have up to 16 Ethernet ports of the same type.  Up to eight ports can be active, and up to eight ports can be in standby mode.

name: link state group
detail: Configure an interface as a member of a link-state group.

params.name: number
params.description: (Optional) Specifies the number of the link-state group. The range is 1 to 2. The default group number is 1.

params.name: downstream
params.description: Configures the interface as a downstream interface in the group.

params.name: upstream
params.description: Configures the interface as an upstream interface in the group.

usageSummary: This command is supported only on the LAN Base image.  Add upstream interfaces to the link-state group before adding downstream interfaces, otherwise, the downstream interfaces move into error-disable mode.  These are the limitations: • An interface can be an upstream interface or a downstream interface.

name: link state track
detail: Enable a link-state group.
usageSummary: This command is supported only on the LAN Base image.  Use the link state group command to create and configure the link-state group.  You then can use this command to enable the link-state group.

name: pagp learn-method
detail: Learn the source address of incoming packets received from an EtherChannel port.

params.name: aggregation-port
params.description: Specifies address learning on the logical port channel. The device sends packets to the source using any port in the EtherChannel. This setting is the default. With aggregation-port learning, it is not important on which physical port the packet arrives.

params.name: physical-port
params.description: Specifies address learning on the physical port within the EtherChannel. The device sends packets to the source using the same port in the EtherChannel from which it learned the source address. The other end of the channel uses the same port in the channel for a particular destination MAC or IP address.

usageSummary: The learn method must be configured the same at both ends of the link.  The device supports address learning only on aggregate ports even though the physical-port keyword is provided in the command-line interface (CLI).  The pagp learn-method and the pagp port-priority interface configuration commands have no effect on the device hardware, but they are required for PAgP interoperability with devices that only support address learning by physical ports.

name: pagp port-priority
detail: Select a port over which all Port Aggregation Protocol (PAgP) traffic through the EtherChannel is sent.

params.name: priority
params.description: Priority number. The range is from 0 to 255.

usageSummary: The physical port with the highest priority that is operational and has membership in the same EtherChannel is the one selected for PAgP transmission.  The device supports address learning only on aggregate ports even though the physical-port keyword is provided in the command-line interface (CLI).  The pagp learn-method and the pagp port-priority interface configuration commands have no effect on the device hardware, but they are required for PAgP interoperability with devices that only support address learning by physical ports, such as the Catalyst 1900 switch.

name: pagp timer
detail: Set the PAgP timer expiration.
usageSummary: This command is available for all interfaces configured as part of a PAgP port channel.  This example shows how to set the PAgP timer expiration to 50 seconds: Switch(config-if)# pagp timer 50 Consolidated Platform Command Reference, Cisco IOS Release 15.

name: port-channel load-balance
detail: Set the load-distribution method among the ports in the EtherChannel.
syntax: port-channel load-balance {dst-ip | dst-mac | src-dst-ip | src-dst-mac | src-ip | src-mac}

params.name: dst-ip
params.description: Specifies load distribution based on the destination host IP address.

params.name: dst-mac
params.description: Specifies load distribution based on the destination host MAC address. Packets to the same destination are sent on the same port, but packets to different destinations are sent on different ports in the channel. src-dst-ip Specifies load distribution based on the source and destination host IP address. src-dst-mac Specifies load distribution based on the source and destination host MAC address.

params.name: src-ip
params.description: Specifies load distribution based on the source host IP address.

params.name: src-mac
params.description: Specifies load distribution based on the source MAC address. Packets from different hosts use different ports in the channel, but packets from the same host use the same port.

usageSummary: You can verify your setting by entering the show running-config privileged EXEC command or the show etherchannel load-balance privileged EXEC command.

name: rep admin vlan
detail: Configure a Resilient Ethernet Protocol (REP) administrative VLAN for REP to transmit hardware flood layer (HFL) messages.
noForm: no rep admin vlan vlan-id [segment segment-id]

params.name: vlan-id
params.description: The REP administrative VLAN. This is a 48-bit static MAC address. segment segment-id Configures the administrative VLAN for the specified segment. The segment ID range is from 1 to 1024.

usageSummary: The range of the REP administrative VLAN is from 2 to 4094.  If you do not configure an administrative VLAN, the default VLAN is VLAN 1.  There can be only one administrative VLAN on a device and on a segment.

name: rep block port
detail: Configure a Resilient Ethernet Protocol (REP) VLAN load balancing on the REP primary edge port.
syntax: rep block port {id port-id | neighbor-offset | preferred} vlan {vlan-list | all}
noForm: no rep block port {id port-id | neighbor-offset | preferred}

params.name: id port-id
params.description: Specifies the VLAN blocking alternate port by entering the unique port ID, which is automatically generated when REP is enabled. The REP port ID is a 16-character hexadecimal value. neighbor-offset Identifies the VLAN blocking alternate port by entering the offset number of a neighbor. The range is from -256 to +256; a value of 0 is invalid.

params.name: preferred
params.description: Selects the regular segment port previously identified as the preferred alternate port for VLAN load balancing.

params.name: vlan
params.description: Identifies the VLANs to be blocked.

params.name: vlan-list
params.description: VLAN ID or range of VLAN IDs to be displayed. Enter a VLAN ID from 1 to 4094 or a range or sequence of VLANs (such as 1-3, 22, 41-44) to be blocked.

params.name: all
params.description: Blocks all VLANs.

usageSummary: You must enter this command on the REP primary edge port.  When you select an alternate port by entering an offset number, this number identifies the downstream neighbor port of an edge port.  The primary edge port has an offset number of 1; positive numbers above 1 identify downstream neighbors of the primary edge port.

name: rep lsl-age-timer
detail: Configure the Resilient Ethernet Protocol (REP) link status layer (LSL) age-out timer value.
usageSummary: The rep lsl-age-timer command is used to configure the REP LSL age-out timer value.  While configuring REP configurable timers, we recommend that you configure the REP LSL number of retries first and then configure the REP LSL age-out timer value.  The following example shows how to configure REP LSL age-out timer value.

name: rep preempt delay
detail: Configure a waiting period after a segment port failure and recovery before Resilient Ethernet Protocol (REP) VLAN load balancing is triggered.
usageSummary: You must enter this command on the REP primary edge port.  You must enter this command and configure a preempt time delay for VLAN load balancing to automatically trigger after a link failure and recovery.  If VLAN load balancing is configured, after a segment port failure and recovery, the REP primary edge port starts a delay timer before VLAN load balancing occurs.

name: rep preempt segment
detail: Manually start Resilient Ethernet Protocol (REP) VLAN load balancing on a segment.

params.name: segment-id
params.description: ID of the REP segment. The range is from 1 to 1024.

usageSummary: Enter this command on the segment, which has the primary edge port on the device.  Ensure that all the other segment configuration is completed before setting preemption for VLAN load balancing.  When you enter the rep preempt segment segment-id command, a confirmation message appears before the command is executed because preemption for VLAN load balancing can disrupt the network.

name: rep segment
detail: Enable Resilient Ethernet Protocol (REP) on an interface and to assign a segment ID to the interface.

params.name: segment-id
params.description: Segment for which REP is enabled. Assign a segment ID to the interface. The range is from 1 to 1024.

params.name: edge
params.description: (Optional) Configures the port as an edge port. Each segment has only two edge ports. no-neighbor (Optional) Specifies the segment edge as one with no external REP neighbor.

params.name: primary
params.description: (Optional) Specifies that the port is the primary edge port where you can configure VLAN load balancing. A segment has only one primary edge port.

params.name: preferred
params.description: (Optional) Specifies that the port is the preferred alternate port or the preferred port for VLAN load balancing.

params.name: Note
params.description: Configuring a port as a preferred port does not guarantee that it becomes the alternate port; it merely gives it a slight edge among equal contenders. The alternate port is usually a previously failed port.

usageSummary: REP ports must be a Layer 2 IEEE 802. 1Q port or 802. 1AD port.

name: rep stcn
detail: Configure a Resilient Ethernet Protocol (REP) edge port to send segment topology change notifications (STCNs) to another interface or to other segments.

params.name: interface interface-id
params.description: Specifies a physical interface or port channel to receive STCNs. segment segment-id-list Specifies one REP segment or a list of segments to receive STCNs. The segment range is from 1 to 1024. You can also configure a sequence of segments (for example 3 to 5, 77, 100).

usageSummary: Enter this command on a segment edge port to send STCNs to one or more segments or to an interface.  You can verify your settings by entering the show interfaces rep detail privileged EXEC command.  The following example shows how to configure a REP edge port to send STCNs to segments 25 to 50.

name: show etherchannel
detail: Display EtherChannel information for a channel.
syntax: show etherchannel [{channel-group-number | {detail | port | port-channel | protocol | summary }}] | [{auto | detail | load-balance | port | port-channel | protocol | summary}]

params.name: channel-group-number
params.description: (Optional) Channel group number. The range is 1 to 24.

params.name: auto
params.description: (Optional) Displays that Etherchannel is created automatically.

params.name: detail
params.description: (Optional) Displays detailed EtherChannel information.

params.name: load-balance
params.description: (Optional) Displays the load-balance or frame-distribution scheme among ports in the port channel.

params.name: port
params.description: (Optional) Displays EtherChannel port information.

params.name: port-channel
params.description: (Optional) Displays port-channel information.

params.name: protocol
params.description: (Optional) Displays the protocol that is being used in the channel.

params.name: summary
params.description: (Optional) Displays a one-line summary per channel group.

usageSummary: If you do not specify a channel group number, all channel groups are displayed.  In the output, the passive port list field is displayed only for Layer 3 port channels.  This field means that the physical port, which is still not up, is configured to be in the channel group (and indirectly is in the only port channel in the channel group).

name: show interfaces rep detail
detail: Display detailed Resilient Ethernet Protocol (REP) configuration and status for all interfaces or a specified interface, including the administrative VLAN.

params.name: interface-id
params.description: (Optional) Physical interface used to display the port ID.

usageSummary: Enter this command on a segment edge port to send STCNs to one or more segments or to an interface.  You can verify your settings by entering the show interfaces rep detail privileged EXEC command.  The following example shows how to display the REP configuration and status for a specified interface.

name: show lacp
detail: Display Link Aggregation Control Protocol (LACP) channel-group information.

params.name: channel-group-number
params.description: (Optional) Channel group number. The range is 1 to 24.

params.name: counters
params.description: Displays traffic information.

params.name: internal
params.description: Displays internal information.

params.name: neighbor
params.description: Displays neighbor information.

params.name: sys-id
params.description: Displays the system identifier that is being used by LACP. The system identifier consists of the LACP system priority and the device MAC address.

usageSummary: You can enter any show lacp command to display the active channel-group information.  To display specific channel information, enter the show lacp command with a channel-group number.  If you do not specify a channel group, information for all channel groups appears.

name: show link state group
detail: Display link-state group information.

params.name: number
params.description: (Optional) Specifies the number of the link-state group number. The range is 1 to 2.

params.name: detail
params.description: (Optional) Displays detailed information about the link-state group.

usageSummary: This command is supported only on the LAN Base image.  To display information about all link-state groups, enter this command without keywords.  To display information about a specific link-state group enter the link-state group number.

name: show pagp
detail: Display Port Aggregation Protocol (PAgP) channel-group information.

params.name: counters
params.description: Displays traffic information.

params.name: dual-active
params.description: Displays the dual-active status.

params.name: internal
params.description: Displays internal information.

params.name: neighbor
params.description: Displays neighbor information.

usageSummary: You can enter any show pagp command to display the active channel-group information.  To display the nonactive information, enter the show pagp command with a channel-group number.

name: show platform backup interface
detail: Display platform-dependent backup information used in a Flex Links configuration.
usageSummary: Use this command only when you are working directly with a technical support representative while troubleshooting a problem.  Do not use this command unless a technical support representative asks you to do so.  Consolidated Platform Command Reference, Cisco IOS Release 15.

name: show platform etherchannel
detail: Display platform-dependent EtherChannel information.

params.name: data-structures
params.description: Displays EtherChannel data structures.

params.name: flags
params.description: Displays EtherChannel port flags.

params.name: time-stamps
params.description: Displays EtherChannel time stamps.

usageSummary: Use this command only when you are working directly with a technical support representative while troubleshooting a problem.  Do not use this command unless a technical support representative asks you to do so.  Consolidated Platform Command Reference, Cisco IOS Release 15.

name: show platform pm
detail: Display platform-dependent port manager information.
syntax: show platform pm {counters | group-masks | idbs {active-idbs | deleted-idbs} | if-numbers | link-status | module-info | platform-block | port-info interface-id | stack-view | vlan {info | line-state}}
usageSummary: The stack-view keyword is not supported on switches running the LAN Lite image.  Use this command only when you are working directly with your technical support representative while troubleshooting a problem.  Do not use this command unless your technical support representative asks you to do so.

name: show platform spanning-tree
detail: Display platform-dependent spanning-tree information.
syntax: show platform spanning-tree synchronization [{detail | vlan vlan-id}]

params.name: detail
params.description: (Optional) Displays detailed spanning-tree information.

params.name: vlan vlan-id
params.description: (Optional) Displays VLAN device spanning-tree information for the specified VLAN. The range is 1 to 4094.

usageSummary: Use this command only when you are working directly with your technical support representative while troubleshooting a problem.  Do not use this command unless your technical support representative asks you to do so.  Consolidated Platform Command Reference, Cisco IOS Release 15.

name: show rep topology
detail: Display Resilient Ethernet Protocol (REP) topology information for a segment or for all segments, including the primary and secondary edge ports in the segment.

params.name: segment segment-id
params.description: (Optional) Specifies the segment for which to display the REP topology information. The segment-id range is from 1 to 1024.

params.name: archive
params.description: (Optional) Displays the previous topology of the segment. This keyword is useful for troubleshooting a link failure.

params.name: detail
params.description: (Optional) Displays detailed REP topology information.

name: show spanning-tree
detail: Display spanning-tree information for the specified spanning-tree instances.
syntax: show spanning-tree [{active | backbonefast | blockedports | bridge | detail | inconsistentports | interface interface-type interface-number | mst | pathcost | root | summary [totals] | uplinkfast | vlan vlan-id}]

params.name: active
params.description: (Optional) Displays spanning-tree information on active interfaces only.

params.name: backbonefast
params.description: (Optional) Displays spanning-tree BackboneFast status.

params.name: blockedports
params.description: (Optional) Displays blocked port information.

params.name: bridge
params.description: (Optional) Displays status and configuration of this switch.

params.name: detail
params.description: (Optional) Displays detailed information.

params.name: inconsistentports
params.description: (Optional) Displays information about inconsistent ports.

params.name: interface interface-type
params.description: (Optional) Specifies the type and number of the interface. interface-number

params.name: mst
params.description: (Optional) Specifies multiple spanning-tree.

params.name: pathcost
params.description: (Optional) Displays spanning-tree pathcost options.

params.name: root
params.description: (Optional) Displays root-switch status and configuration.

params.name: summary
params.description: (Optional) Specifies a summary of port states.

params.name: totals
params.description: (Optional) Displays the total lines of the spanning-tree state section.

params.name: uplinkfast
params.description: (Optional) Displays spanning-tree UplinkFast status.

params.name: vlan vlan-id
params.description: (Optional) Specifies the VLAN ID. The range is 1 to 4094.

usageSummary: If you do not specify a vlan-id value when you use the vlan keyword, the command applies to spanning-tree instances for all VLANs.  This is an example of output from the show spannning-tree active command: Consolidated Platform Command Reference, Cisco IOS Release 15. 2(6)E (Catalyst 2960-X Switches) 179 Layer 2 show spanning-tree Device# show spanning-tree active VLAN0001 Spanning tree enabled protocol ieee Root ID Priority 32768 Address 0001.

name: show udld
detail: Display UniDirectional Link Detection (UDLD) administrative and operational status for all ports or the specified port.

params.name: interface-id
params.description: (Optional) ID of the interface and port number. Valid interfaces include physical ports, VLANs, and port channels.

params.name: neighbors
params.description: (Optional) Displays neighbor information only.

usageSummary: If you do not enter an interface ID, administrative and operational UDLD status for all interfaces appear.  This is an example of output from the show udld interface-id command.  For this display, UDLD is enabled on both ends of the link, and UDLD detects that the link is bidirectional.

name: spanning-tree backbonefast
detail: Enable BackboneFast to allow a blocked port on a device to change immediately to a listening mode.
usageSummary: Enable BackboneFast so that the device detects indirect link failures and starts the spanning-tree reconfiguration sooner than it would under normal spanning-tree rules.  You can configure BackboneFast for rapid PVST+ or for multiple spanning-tree (MST) mode; however, the feature remains disabled until you change the spanning-tree mode to PVST+.  Use the show spanning-tree privileged EXEC command to verify your settings.

name: spanning-tree bpdufilter
detail: Enable bridge protocol data unit (BPDU) filtering on the interface.

params.name: enable
params.description: Enables BPDU filtering on this interface.

params.name: disable
params.description: Disables BPDU filtering on this interface.

usageSummary: This command has three states: • spanning-tree bpdufilter enable —Unconditionally enables BPDU filtering on the interface.  • spanning-tree bpdufilter disable —Unconditionally disables BPDU filtering on the interface.  • no spanning-tree bpdufilter —Enables BPDU filtering on the interface if the interface is in the operational PortFast state and if you configure the spanning-tree portfast bpdufilter default command.

name: spanning-tree bpduguard
detail: Enable bridge protocol data unit (BPDU) guard on the interface.

params.name: enable
params.description: Enables BPDU guard on this interface.

params.name: disable
params.description: Disables BPDU guard on this interface.

usageSummary: Use the BPDU guard feature in a service-provider environment to prevent an access port from participating in the spanning tree.  If the port still receives a BPDU, it is put in the error-disabled state as a protective measure.  This command has three states: • spanning-tree bpduguard enable —Unconditionally enables BPDU guard on the interface.

name: spanning-tree bridge assurance
detail: Enable Bridge Assurance on your network.
usageSummary: This feature protects your network from bridging loops.  It monitors the receipt of BPDUs on point-to-point links on all network ports.  When a port does not receive BPDUs within the allotted hello time period, the port is put into a blocked state (the same as a port inconsistent state, which stops forwarding of frames).

name: spanning-tree cost
detail: Set the path cost of the interface for Spanning Tree Protocol (STP) calculations.

params.name: cost
params.description: The path cost; valid values are from 1 to 200000000.

usageSummary: When you specify VLANs associated with a spanning tree instance, you can specify a single VLAN identified by a VLAN ID number, a range of VLANs separated by a hyphen, or a series of VLAN IDs separated by a comma.  When you specify a value for the cost argument, higher values indicate higher costs.  This range applies regardless of the protocol type specified.

name: spanning-tree etherchannel guard misconfig
detail: Display an error message when the device detects an EtherChannel misconfiguration.
usageSummary: When the device detects an EtherChannel misconfiguration, this error message is displayed: PM-4-ERR_DISABLE: Channel-misconfig error detected on [chars], putting [chars] in err-disable state.  To determine which local ports are involved in the misconfiguration, enter the show interfaces status err-disabled command.  To check the EtherChannel configuration on the remote device, enter the show etherchannel summary command on the remote device.

name: spanning-tree extend system-id
detail: Enable extended system identification.
usageSummary: The spanning tree uses the extended system ID, the device priority, and the allocated spanning-tree MAC address to make the bridge ID unique for each VLAN or multiple spanning-tree instance.  Because a switch stack appears as a single switch to the rest of the network, all switches in the stack use the same bridge ID for a given spanning tree.  If the stack's active switch fails, the stack members recalculate their bridge IDs of all running spanning trees based on the new MAC address of the stack's active switch.

name: spanning-tree guard
detail: Enable or disable root-guard mode or loop-guard mode on the VLANs associated with an interface.

params.name: loop
params.description: Enables the loop-guard mode on the interface.

params.name: root
params.description: Enables root-guard mode on the interface.

params.name: none
params.description: Sets the guard mode to none.

usageSummary: You can enable root guard or loop guard when the device is operating in the per-VLAN spanning-tree plus (PVST+), rapid-PVST+, or the multiple spanning-tree (MST) mode.  You cannot enable both root guard and loop guard at the same time.  Use the spanning-tree guard loop command to override the setting of the spanning-tree loop guard default setting.

name: spanning-tree link-type
detail: Configure a link type for a port.

params.name: shared
params.description: Specifies that the interface is a shared medium.

usageSummary: Rapid Spanning Tree Protocol Plus (RSTP+) fast transition works only on point-to-point links between two bridges.  By default, the device derives the link type of a port from the duplex mode.  A full-duplex port is considered as a point-to-point link while a half-duplex configuration is assumed to be on a shared link.

name: spanning-tree loopguard default
detail: Enable loop guard as a default on all ports of a given bridge.
usageSummary: Loop guard provides additional security in the bridge network.  Loop guard prevents alternate or root ports from becoming the designated port due to a failure that could lead to a unidirectional link.  Loop guard operates only on ports that are considered point-to-point by the spanning tree.

name: spanning-tree mode
detail: Switch between per-VLAN Spanning Tree+ (PVST+), Rapid-PVST+, and Multiple Spanning Tree (MST) modes.

params.name: pvst
params.description: Enables PVST+ mode.

params.name: mst
params.description: Enables MST mode.

params.name: rapid-pvst
params.description: Enables Rapid-PVST+ mode.

usageSummary: Only one mode can be active at a time.  All stack members run the same spanning-tree mode.  Caution Be careful when using the spanning-tree mode command to switch between PVST+, Rapid-PVST+, and MST modes.

name: spanning-tree mst configuration
detail: Enter MST-configuration mode.
usageSummary: You can use these commands for MST configuration: • abort Exits the MST region configuration mode without applying configuration changes.  • exit Exits the MST region configuration mode and applies all configuration changes.  • instance instance_id vlan vlan_id Maps VLANs to an MST instance.

name: spanning-tree mst cost
detail: Set the path cost of the interface for multiple spanning tree (MST) calculations.

params.name: instance-id
params.description: Range of spanning-tree instances. The range is 1 to 4094.

params.name: cost
params.description: Path cost. The range is 1 to 200000000.

usageSummary: When you specify a value for the cost argument, higher values indicate higher costs.

name: spanning-tree mst forward-time
detail: Set the forward-delay timer for MST instances.

params.name: seconds
params.description: Number of seconds to set the forward-delay timer for all the MST instances. The range is 4 to 30.

name: spanning-tree mst hello-time
detail: Set the hello-time delay timer.

params.name: seconds
params.description: Interval, in seconds, between hello BPDUs. The range is 1 to 10.

usageSummary: If you do not specify the hello-time value, the value is calculated from the network diameter.  Exercise care when using this command.  For most situations, we recommend that you use the spanning-tree vlan vlan-id root primary and the spanning-tree vlan vlan-id root secondary global configuration commands to modify the hello time.

name: spanning-tree mst max-age
detail: Set the interval between messages that the spanning tree receives from the root switch.

params.name: seconds
params.description: Interval, in seconds, between messages the spanning tree receives from the root switch. The range is 6 to 40.

name: spanning-tree mst max-hops
detail: Specify the number of possible hops in the region before a bridge protocol data unit (BPDU) is discarded.

params.name: hop-count
params.description: Number of possible hops in the region before a BPDU is discarded. The range is 1 to 255.

name: spanning-tree mst port-priority
detail: Set the priority for an interface.

params.name: instance-id
params.description: Range of spanning-tree instances. The range is 1 to 4094.

params.name: priority
params.description: Priority. The range is 0 to 240 in increments of 16.

usageSummary: You can assign higher priority values (lower numerical values) to interfaces that you want selected first and lower priority values (higher numerical values) that you want selected last.  If all interfaces have the same priority value, the multiple spanning tree (MST) puts the interface with the lowest interface number in the forwarding state and blocks other interfaces.  If the switch is a member of a switch stack, you must use the spanning-tree mst instance_id cost cost command to select an interface to put in the forwarding state.

name: spanning-tree mst pre-standard
detail: Configure a port to transmit only prestandard bridge protocol data units (BPDUs).
usageSummary: The port can accept both prestandard and standard BPDUs.  If the neighbor types are mismatched, only the common and internal spanning tree (CIST) runs on this interface.

name: spanning-tree mst priority
detail: Set the bridge priority for an instance.

params.name: instance
params.description: Instance identification number. The range is 0 to 4094.

params.name: priority priority
params.description: Specifies the bridge priority. The range is 0 to 614440 in increments of 4096.

usageSummary: You can set the bridge priority in increments of 4096 only.  Valid values are 0, 4096, 8192, 12288, 16384, 20480.  24576, 28672, 32768, 40960, 45056, 49152, 53248, 57344 and 61440.

name: spanning-tree mst root
detail: Designate the primary and secondary root switch and set the timer value for an instance.

params.name: instance
params.description: Instance identification number. The range is 0 to 4094.

params.name: primary
params.description: Forces this switch to be the root switch.

params.name: secondary
params.description: Specifies this switch to act as the root switch, if the primary root fail.

usageSummary: Use this command only on backbone switches.  You can enter instance-id as a single instance or a range of instances, for example, 0-3,5,7-9.  When you enter the spanning-tree mst instance-id root command, the software tries to set a high enough priority to make this switch the root of the spanning-tree instance.

name: spanning-tree mst simulate pvst
detail: Enable PVST + simulation globally.
noForm: no spanning-tree mst simulate pvst global
usageSummary: This feature configures MST switches (in the same region) to seamlessly interact with PVST+ switches.  Use the show spanning-tree summary command to see if the feature is enabled.  To enable PVST+ simulation on a port, see spanning-tree mst simulate pvst (interface configuration).

name: spanning-tree mst simulate pvst
detail: Enable PVST + simulation on a port.

params.name: disable
params.description: Disables the PVST+ simulation feature. This prevents a port from automatically interoperating with a connecting device that is running Rapid PVST+.

usageSummary: This feature configures MST switches (in the same region) to seamlessly interact with PVST+ switches.  Use the show spanning-tree interface interface-id detail command to see if the feature is enabled.  To enable PVST+ simulation globally, see spanning-tree mst simulate pvst global.

name: spanning-tree pathcost method
detail: Set the default path-cost calculation method.

params.name: long
params.description: Specifies the 32-bit based values for default port-path costs.

params.name: short
params.description: Specifies the 16-bit based values for default port-path costs.

usageSummary: The long path-cost calculation method utilizes all 32 bits for path-cost calculation and yields values in the range of 1 through 200,000,000.  The short path-cost calculation method (16 bits) yields values in the range of 1 through 65535.

name: spanning-tree port-priority
detail: Configure an interface priority when two bridges tie for position as the root bridge.
usageSummary: The priority you set breaks the tie.

name: spanning-tree portfast edge
detail: Enable bridge protocol data unit (BPDU) filtering on PortFast edge-enabled interfaces, the BDPU guard feature on PortFast edge-enabled interfaces, or the PortFast edge feature on all nontrunking interfaces.
syntax: spanning-tree portfast edge {bpdufilter default | bpduguard default | default}
noForm: no portfast edge {bpdufilter default | bpduguard default | default}

params.name: bdpufilter default
params.description: Enables BDPU filtering on PortFast edge-enabled interfaces and prevents the switch interface connect to end stations from sending or receiving BPDUs. bdpuguard default Enables the BDPU guard feature on PortFast edge-enabled interfaces and places the interfaces that receive BPDUs in an error-disabled state.

params.name: default
params.description: Enables the PortFast edge feature on all nontrunking interfaces.

usageSummary: You can enable these features when the switch is operating in the per-VLAN spanning-tree plus (PVST+) rapid-PVST+, or the multiple spanning-tree (MST) mode.  Use the spanning-tree portfast edge bpdufilter default global configuration command to globally enable BPDU filtering on interfaces that are PortFast edge-enabled (the interfaces are in a PortFast edge-operational state).  The interfaces still send a few BPDUs at link-up before the switch begins to filter outbound BPDUs.

name: spanning-tree portfast edge
detail: Enable PortFast edge mode where the interface is immediately put into the forwarding state upon linkup without waiting for the timer to expire.
usageSummary: You can enable this feature when the switch is operating in the per-VLAN spanning-tree plus (PVST+), Rapid PVST+, or the multiple spanning-tree (MST) mode.  This feature affects all VLANs on the interface.  Use this command only on interfaces that connect to end stations; otherwise, an accidental topology loop could cause a data-packet loop and disrupt the switch and network operation.

name: spanning-tree transmit hold-count
detail: Specify the transmit hold count.

params.name: value
params.description: Number of bridge protocol data units (BPDUs) sent every second. The range is 1 to 20.

usageSummary: This command is supported on all spanning-tree modes.  The transmit hold count determines the number of BPDUs that can be sent before pausing for 1 second.

name: spanning-tree uplinkfast
detail: Enable UplinkFast.

params.name: max-update-rate
params.description: (Optional) Specifies the rate (number of packets per second) at which

params.name: packets-per-second
params.description: update packets are sent. The range is 0 to 320000. The default is 150.

usageSummary: Use this command only on access switches.  You can configure the UplinkFast feature for rapid PVST+ or for multiple spanning-tree (MST) mode, but the feature remains disabled (inactive) until you change the spanning-tree mode to PVST+.  When you enable UplinkFast, it is enabled for the entire switch; it cannot be enabled for individual VLANs.

name: spanning-tree vlan
detail: Configure Spanning Tree Protocol (STP) on a per-virtual LAN (VLAN) basis.
syntax: spanning-tree vlan vlan-id [{forward-time seconds | hello-time seconds | max-age seconds | priority priority | [root {primary | secondary} [diameter net-diameter]]}]
noForm: no spanning-tree vlan vlan-id [{forward-time | hello-time | max-age | priority | root}]

params.name: vlan-id
params.description: VLAN range associated with the spanning-tree instance. The range is 1 to 4094.

params.name: forward-time seconds
params.description: (Optional) Sets the STP forward delay time in second. The range is 4 to 30. The default is 15.

params.name: hello-time seconds
params.description: (Optional) Specifies the duration, in seconds, between the generation of configuration messages by the root switch. The range is 1 to 10. The default is 2.

params.name: max-age seconds
params.description: (Optional) Sets the maximum number of seconds the information in a bridge packet data unit (BPDU) is valid. The range is 6 to 40. The default is 20.

params.name: priority priority
params.description: (Optional) Sets the STP bridge priority. The range is 0 to 61440 in increments of 4096. The default for the primary root switch is 24576. The default for the secondary root switch is 28672.

params.name: root primary
params.description: (Optional) Forces this switch to be the root switch.

params.name: root secondary
params.description: (Optional) Specifies this switch to act as the root switch should the primary root fail. diameter net -diameter (Optional) Specifies the maximum number of switches between any two points of attachment of end stations. The range is 2 through 7.

usageSummary: If the switch does not hear BPDUs within the time specified by the max-age seconds- value, it recomputes the spanning-tree topology.  Use the spanning-tree vlan vlan-id root only on backbone switches.  Consolidated Platform Command Reference, Cisco IOS Release 15.

name: switchport access vlan
detail: Configure a port as a static-access port.

params.name: vlan-id
params.description: (Optional) Number of the VLAN on the interface in access mode. Valid values are from 1 to 4094.

usageSummary: The port must be in access mode before the switchport access vlan command can take effect.  If the switchport mode is set to access vlan vlan-id, the port operates as a member of the specified VLAN.  If set to access vlan dynamic, the port starts discovery of VLAN assignment based on the incoming packets it receives.

name: switchport mode
detail: Configure the VLAN membership mode of a port.
syntax: switchport mode {access | dynamic | {auto | desirable} | trunk} noswitchport mode {access | dynamic | {auto | desirable} | trunk}

params.name: access
params.description: Sets the port to access mode (either static-access or dynamic-access depending on the setting of the switchport access vlan interface configuration command). The port is set to access unconditionally and operates as a nontrunking, single VLAN interface that sends and receives nonencapsulated (non-tagged) frames. An access port can be assigned to only one VLAN.

params.name: dynamic auto
params.description: Sets the port trunking mode dynamic parameter to auto to specify that the interface convert the link to a trunk link. This is the default switchport mode.

params.name: dynamic
params.description: Sets the port trunking mode dynamic parameter to desirable to specify that the interface

params.name: desirable
params.description: actively attempt to convert the link to a trunk link.

params.name: trunk
params.description: Sets the port to trunk unconditionally. The port is a trunking VLAN Layer 2 interface. The port sends and receives encapsulated (tagged) frames that identify the VLAN of origination. A trunk is a point-to-point link between two devices or between a device and a router.

usageSummary: A configuration that uses the access,or trunk keywords takes effect only when you configure the port in the appropriate mode by using the switchport mode command.  The static-access and trunk configuration are saved, but only one configuration is active at a time.  When you enter access mode, the interface changes to permanent nontrunking mode and negotiates to convert the link into a nontrunk link even if the neighboring interface does not agree to the change.

name: switchport nonegotiate
detail: Specify that Dynamic Trunking Protocol (DTP) negotiation packets are not sent on the Layer 2 interface.
usageSummary: The no switchport nonegotiate command removes nonegotiate status.  This command is valid only when the interface switchport mode is access or trunk (configured by using the switchport mode access or the switchport mode trunk interface configuration command).  This command returns an error if you attempt to execute it in dynamic (auto or desirable) mode.

name: udld
detail: Enable aggressive or normal mode in the UniDirectional Link Detection (UDLD) and to set the configurable message timer time.

params.name: aggressive
params.description: Enables UDLD in aggressive mode on all fiber-optic interfaces.

params.name: enable
params.description: Enables UDLD in normal mode on all fiber-optic interfaces.

params.name: message time
params.description: Configures the period of time between UDLD probe messages on ports

params.name: message-timer-interval
params.description: that are in the advertisement phase and are determined to be bidirectional. The range is 1 to 90 seconds. The default is 15 seconds.

usageSummary: UDLD supports two modes of operation: normal (the default) and aggressive.  In normal mode, UDLD detects unidirectional links due to misconnected interfaces on fiber-optic connections.  In aggressive mode, UDLD also detects unidirectional links due to one-way traffic on fiber-optic and twisted-pair links and due to misconnected interfaces on fiber-optic links.

name: udld port
detail: Enable UniDirectional Link Detection (UDLD) on an individual interface or to prevent a fiber-optic interface from being enabled by the udld global configuration command.

params.name: aggressive
params.description: (Optional) Enables UDLD in aggressive mode on the specified interface.

usageSummary: A UDLD-capable port cannot detect a unidirectional link if it is connected to a UDLD-incapable port of another device.  UDLD supports two modes of operation: normal (the default) and aggressive.  In normal mode, UDLD detects unidirectional links due to misconnected interfaces on fiber-optic connections.

name: udld reset
detail: Reset all interfaces disabled by UniDirectional Link Detection (UDLD) and permit traffic to begin passing through them again (though other features, such as spanning tree, Port Aggregation Protocol (PAgP), and Dynamic Trunking Protocol (DTP) still have their normal effects, if enabled).
usageSummary: If the interface configuration is still enabled for UDLD, these ports begin to run UDLD again and are disabled for the same reason if the problem has not been corrected.  This example shows how to reset all interfaces disabled by UDLD: Device# udld reset 1 ports shutdown by UDLD were reset.  Related Topics show udld, on page 183 udld, on page 230 udld port, on page 232 Consolidated Platform Command Reference, Cisco IOS Release 15.
